title: Directional momentum alignment
statement: When both short-term (5m) and medium-term (1h) directional returns are positive in sign-flipped feature space (aligned with the candidate event side), the event achieves higher mean net_R than random selection on untouched out-of-sample data.

decision_rule.description: TAKE when return_5m > 0 AND return_1h > 0, ABSTAIN otherwise. Both features are sign-flipped for SHORT events so positive always means aligned with event direction.

falsification.criterion: H1 mean_net_r on frozen test split is not greater than zero AND not greater than always-take baseline mean_net_r
falsification.pass: H1 test mean_net_r > 0 AND H1 test mean_net_r > always-take test mean_net_r
falsification.fail: H1 test mean_net_r <= 0 AND H1 test mean_net_r <= always-take test mean_net_r
falsification.hold: insufficient evidence (e.g. zero trades taken, or positive but ambiguous vs baselines)

negative_controls: shuffled-label: re-fit linear OLS on shuffled train labels, evaluate on frozen test — 10 shuffles

data.source: Phase 4 pilot universe (OKX, 10 symbols, early profile, 3 years)
data.setup: HunterSpec V0: k_stop=2.0, reward_risk=3.0, 1h decision on 5m sim
